title: erycibe subspicata
intro: Erycibe subspicata, also known as the Pusa or Pusa vine, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Convolvulaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Erycibe subspicata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
